response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and 13+ years of experience, they lack the core C++ specialization this role demands. With only 6 months of documented C++ work at National Instruments, their career is heavily weighted toward web services, Node.js, and full-stack development rather than systems programming and industrial automation. The absence of any mention of industrial cybersecurity standards (SDL, IEC 62443), multi-threaded systems programming, MQTT, message brokers, or embedded systems further indicates misalignment with this position's technical focus.
strengths: Demonstrates strong software craftsmanship through consistent application of TDD, CI/CD, and clean architecture principles across multiple roles
Brings extensive full-stack development expertise including Node.js and React—both listed as advantageous skills for the role
Proven track record of mentorship and community contribution through technical workshops, open-source projects (GISCollective, Trial), and knowledge sharing
concerns: Severely limited C++ depth—only 6 months documented at National Instruments, insufficient for a mid-senior architect role requiring advanced C++, STL, and systems-level design
No evidence of industrial automation, IoT, embedded systems, or cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443, SDL) experience despite these being core to the position
Career progression shows consistent focus on web services and modern full-stack development rather than real-time networked systems or concurrent programming

key_factors.factor: C++ specialization and systems programming depth
Industrial automation and security standards knowledge
Software engineering maturity and testing practices
